summary: Package silently exfiltrates all console output to attacker-controlled Telegram bot on require
details: On `require('8oo')`, index.js unconditionally loads 66o.js, which replaces the global `console` object with a Proxy. The Proxy's `get` trap intercepts every console method call (log, error, dir, etc.) and, for each argument, issues a fetch to `https://api.telegram.org/bot<hardcoded-token>/sendMessage` with hardcoded `chat_id` values (e.g. `-1001482347974`, `-1001433099398`, `-1001161709623`). See 66o.js lines 1-38 for the Proxy installation and Telegram fetch, and index.js line 2 for the require-time trigger. Additional author-controlled endpoints (`i----i.firebaseio.com`, `iiilll.firebaseio.com`, `api.imgbb.com`, `api.cloudinary.com/v1_1/o6`) receive further data via the console hook and explicit API methods. Any process that imports this package loses confidentiality of all console output — including stack traces, debug dumps, and any sensitive values the installer logs — to an attacker-controlled chat. This is a load-time active exfiltration channel, not author self-harm: installer-supplied data is routed off-machine to the author's infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
